beskrivelse: The classic half-hour of moderate walking that health authorities recommend. No requirements, no technique — just get out the door and walk at a pace that feels good.

kilde.navn: Nasjonale faglige råd om fysisk aktivitet (Helsedirektoratet, 2022 / WHO 2020)
kilde.notat: The weekly recommendation (150 min of moderate activity) operationalized as one daily 30-min moderate session; an explicit warm-up and cool-down phase added for the timer format.

parametre.sone: Z1–Z2 · RPE 3–4 · you can talk the whole way

blokker.formatNavn: Easy walk — slow the pace gradually

beskrivelse: The viral incline half-hour, in an outdoor version. The hill does the work for you — hold one steady pace uphill and feel your heart rate and breathing respond.

kilde.navn: 12-3-30 (Lauren Giraldo, 2019)
kilde.notat: Adapted from the treadmill (12% incline / 3 mph) to a steady outdoor hill; the work phase shortened from 30 to 20 min and framed with a warm-up/cool-down. The hill controls the intensity — no pacing skill required.

blokker.formatNavn: Steady uphill at a set, unchanging pace

parametre.sone: Z3–Z4 · RPE 7–8 · clearly out of breath, short sentences

blokker.formatNavn: Easy walk on flat ground — get your breath down

beskrivelse: A brisk walk where your breathing is your compass. Find the pace where you can still speak in full sentences — that's the sweet, easy zone that builds endurance without wearing you out.

kilde.navn: Talk Test (Foster/Persinger, University of Wisconsin–La Crosse, ~2004)
kilde.notat: The talk test is an intensity method, not a ready-made session; here it's structured as 3×8 min of holding the zone with 2 min of breathing room — the intervals give natural checkpoints to calibrate your conversational pace.

blokker.formatNavn: Easy walk — so easy you could sing

faser.navn: Brisk walk at conversational pace — full sentences, but not singing

beskrivelse: The Japanese interval method that gave older exercisers stronger legs and lower blood pressure than ordinary walking. Three minutes on, three minutes off — simple, tough and well documented.

kilde.navn: Interval Walking Training (Nose/Masuki, Shinshu University; Nemoto m.fl., Mayo Clinic Proceedings 2007)
kilde.notat: The core protocol (5×3/3) is unchanged from the study; 5 min warm-up and cool-down added (the study specifies only the interval part). 70%/40% of VO2peak translated to RPE/zone feel since the app doesn't measure VO2.

faser.navn: Brisk walk — RPE 7, clearly out of breath, long strides and active arms

beskrivelse: Learn the Finnish Nordic walking technique step by step at an easy pace. Once the poles click, you engage the whole upper body — without your heart rate having to spike.

kilde.navn: INWA 10 Step Method (International Nordic Walking Federation, Finland 2000)
kilde.notat: INWA's 10-step curriculum (normally instructor-led over several sessions) condensed to the first three fundamentals (posture, pull, plant) in one self-guided timer session; the dynamic steps left out to keep the intensity easy. Skill is high because of the technique coordination, not the fitness demand.

blokker.formatNavn: Walking with dangling poles — let them trail behind you, find a natural diagonal arm swing
Technique: plant the pole angled back as your hand passes the hip, open the hand behind you
Flowing Nordic walking — active push-off through the pole, rotate the upper body
Easy walk — loosen shoulders and neck

beskrivelse: Military-inspired marching with weight on your back. The pack turns ordinary walking into strength and cardio in one — start with around 10% of your body weight and feel your whole body work.

kilde.navn: Ruck march (US Army-standard; GORUCK, Jason McCarthy 2008)
kilde.notat: A military continuous long-march (2–3 h) shortened to 4×4 min tempo intervals with rests for the timer format and a civilian audience; the load reduced from the military ~16 kg to ~10% of body weight (GORUCK/Cleveland Clinic recommendation for beginners).

blokker.formatNavn: Easy walk with the pack — feel it sitting high and snug

faser.navn: Fast march-walking with the pack — push the pace, short quick steps on the climb
Easy walk — roll the shoulders, lower the heart rate

blokker.formatNavn: Easy walk — feel free to drop the pack for the last part

beskrivelse: The classic start to your running adventure: you jog only when the beep tells you to, and walk the rest. Millions have started right here — all you need to do is follow the timer.

kilde.navn: Couch to 5K, uke 1 (Josh Clark, 1996; NHS Better Health)
kilde.notat: The Week 1 session used unchanged (5 min walk, 8×[60 s jog + 90 s walk], 5 min walk — identical in the NHS and c25k.com versions). The phase texts were rewritten in our own words; no proprietary coaching text reused.

parametre.sone: Brisk walk · warm up the legs

beskrivelse: A Danish researchers' favorite: 30 easy, 20 a little faster, 10 all-out — over and over. No stopwatch math, just follow the beep and go hard for ten seconds at a time.

kilde.navn: 10-20-30-konseptet (Gunnarsson & Bangsbo, Københavns Universitet, 2012)
kilde.notat: The study used 3–4 blocks of 5 min; 3 chosen for ~30 min total. The three series and the 2-min rests sit as phases within one main block for seamless playback. Low skill: the beep controls everything, the sprint is 'all-out' with no zone-holding. RPE peaks at 9.

beskrivelse: The foundation of all endurance training: a steady, comfortable run where you can talk the entire time. It feels 'too easy' — and that's exactly the point. This is how the pros build their engine.

kilde.navn: Lavintensiv sone 1–2-trening (Seiler & Tønnessen, 2009; talk test: Foster & Porcari)
kilde.notat: A continuous session framed as three timer phases (warm-up/main part/cool-down). Medium skill: requires holding a single zone by feel using the talk test as your tool, but no interval pacing. RPE 3–4.

parametre.sone: Z1–Z2 · full sentences should be possible

beskrivelse: The world's most famous fartlek: the efforts get shorter and sharper while the recoveries shrink in step. Moneghetti ran it every week throughout his career — 20 minutes that give everything.

kilde.navn: Mona-fartlek (Chris Wardlaw for Steve Moneghetti, 1983; fartlek-arv fra Gösta Holmér)
kilde.notat: The original 20-min structure kept exactly; 5 min warm-up added, and an easy cool-down jog baked in as the last phase of the 15/15 block to keep the session playable in five blocks. 'Hard' and 'float' are guided by feel with no exact pace requirements. RPE 7–9.

beskrivelse: The art of running slowly enough: you keep your heart rate under a strict cap and let your aerobic engine do the work. Calculate 180 minus your age before you start — and walk rather than break the cap.

kilde.navn: MAF-metoden / 180-formelen (Dr. Phil Maffetone, 1980-tallet)
kilde.notat: Maffetone's recommended 12–15 min warm-up condensed to 10 min for the app format. High skill despite the easy intensity: it requires a heart-rate monitor and active restraint to keep your heart rate under the cap (180 − age), especially on hills. RPE 2–4.

parametre.sone: From walking to jogging · up toward MAF heart rate

blokker.formatNavn: Steady run under MAF heart rate

parametre.sone: At/just under 180 − age · never above, walk if needed

beskrivelse: Norway's most documented session: four times four minutes where you breathe hard but hold a steady pace all the way. NTNU research shows few sessions raise your fitness faster.

kilde.navn: 4×4-intervaller (Helgerud, Hoff m.fl., CERG/NTNU, 2007)
kilde.notat: CERG's official setup (10 min warm-up, 4×4 min at 85–95% of max heart rate, 3 min active recoveries, 5 min down) used unchanged. High skill: requires steady pacing through the whole 4-minute effort — don't start too hard. RPE 8.

parametre.sone: Up toward 60–70% of max heart rate

faser.navn: Hard and steady — 85–95% of max heart rate

beskrivelse: Gentle chair yoga that loosens the neck, back and hips without getting down on the floor. A perfect break in your day – everything is done at your own pace, seated safely on the chair.

kilde.navn: Sit 'N' Fit Chair Yoga (Kristine Lee / Park et al., 2017)
kilde.notat: The 45-min format from the RCT study is shortened to 20 min with the same selection and order of exercises. Stop if it hurts; skip the twist if your back is sore. For more challenge: hold each pose 2–3 breaths longer, or stand up between rounds.

ovelser.beskrivelse: Rolige runder i pustetempo, sittende på stolen.

beskrivelse: A simple flow at a brisk breathing pace – just standing movements you'll learn in a minute. It lifts your heart rate, warms the back and posterior chain, and builds the rhythm between breath and movement.

kilde.navn: Ardha Surya Namaskar – halv solhilsen (vinyasa-/Ashtanga-tradisjonen, Krishnamacharya-linjen)
kilde.notat: Only the standing part of the sun salutation – without plank/chaturanga/dog. Bend your knees in the forward fold; hands on your shins in the halfway lift. Easier: fewer rounds and a slower pace. Harder: 6 blocks or add a heel raise in mountain pose.

ovelser.beskrivelse: Helt rolige runder, 3–4 pust per stilling.

blokker.formatNavn: Half sun salutation at breathing pace

ovelser.beskrivelse: Én bevegelse per pust. 4 blokker à 5 runder – 30–45 sek pause i fjellstilling mellom blokkene.

beskrivelse: Deep, quiet holds that release tension in the hips and groin. You sink gradually into each pose – the breath does the work, not the muscles. Lovely after running or long days.

kilde.navn: Yin yoga – hofteserie (Paul Grilley / Sarah Powers, videreført av Bernie Clark)
kilde.notat: Basic yin with 2–3 min holds and 30–60 sec of rest on your back between poses. Cushion under the knee/hip in swan; dragon with hands on blocks. Never sharp pain – find 60–70% of your max and stay there. Shorter holds (90 sec) for beginners.

blokker.formatNavn: Yin holds – hip openers (rest 30–60 s between)

beskrivelse: Classic vinyasa: five sun salutations that build heat, then strong warrior poses that hold it. A flowing pace, steady breath – you'll feel both strength and calm afterward.

kilde.navn: Surya Namaskara A + stående sekvens (K. Pattabhi Jois, Ashtanga-tradisjonen)
kilde.notat: The traditional 5 rounds of Sun A plus a standard standing block (warrior 2/triangle family, 5 breaths per pose). Chaturanga on the knees and cobra instead of upward dog as needed; a shorter stance in the warrior poses. Harder: 6–8 rounds of Sun A.

ovelser.beskrivelse: Én bevegelse per pust, rolig flyt.
To runder per side – bytt side hver runde. 5 pust per stilling (3 i fredfull kriger).

navn: Deep Yin with Props – Back and Front
beskrivelse: Deep yin for the experienced: long holds in saddle, seal and snail with good support. The spine bends both ways, the nervous system settles – a quiet, powerful practice.

kilde.navn: Avansert yin med props – sfinx/sel/sadel-serie (Grilley/Powers-tradisjonen, dok. Bernie Clark)
kilde.notat: Saddle requires healthy knees – half saddle or sphinx as a substitute. Never snail with neck problems (switch to lying knees-to-chest). More cushion/blanket = gentler; less = deeper. 30–60 sec of quiet rest between poses.

blokker.formatNavn: Yin – forward fold and front body (rest 30–60 s between)

ovelser.navn: Sneglen med brettet teppe under skuldrene

navn: Sun Salutation B with Warrior Transitions
beskrivelse: A powerful Ashtanga-inspired session: five rounds of Sun Salutation B with warrior transitions, then long holds and crow prep. Steady, deep breath through it all – strength meets flow.

kilde.navn: Surya Namaskara B – 17 vinyasas (K. Pattabhi Jois, Ashtanga-tradisjonen)
kilde.notat: Full traditional B with a chaturanga transition between the warrior sides. Chaturanga on the knees and cobra as needed; 3 rounds of B instead of 5. Skip crow prep if your wrists bother you (hold plank for 5 breaths instead). Harder: full crow and 5 breaths in each downward dog.

ovelser.beskrivelse: Rolige runder for å bygge varme.
Én bevegelse per pust – kriger 1 med chaturanga-overgang per side.

beskrivelse: A safe and simple full-body session built around the goblet squat. You learn the four basic patterns with light weights and plenty of margin – a perfect start to strength training at home.

kilde.navn: Goblet-grunnprogram (Dan John, 2011)
kilde.notat: Dan John's template is a principle (squat/press/pull/carry), not a fixed program; the doses set conservatively (RIR 4–5) for easy intensity. The carry can be swapped for a standing hold if space is tight. The dumbbell can be swapped for a kettlebell.

beskrivelse: The classic that started it all: three ascending sets where only the last is heavy. You warm up within the exercise itself and give everything at the end – simple, brutally effective and easy to control.

kilde.navn: Progressive Resistance Exercise (DeLorme & Watkins, 1948)
kilde.notat: The original's last set goes to 10RM (RIR 0); here it stops at RIR 1–2 for safety training alone at home. The 50/75/100% ramp is preserved. The original was machine-/rehab-based; adapted here to simple dumbbell exercises.

blokker.formatNavn: DeLorme ramp · last set heavy, stop at RIR 1–2

navn: ACSM Full Body – Technique and Hinge
beskrivelse: An easy full-body session following the ACSM recipe, with the hip hinge and press in focus. Light weights, plenty of time and full control – you build the technique that later lets you lift heavy.

kilde.navn: ACSM Position Stand: Progression Models in Resistance Training (ACSM, 2009/2026)
kilde.notat: ACSM specifies 8–12 reps to volitional fatigue; the easy variant uses the same exercise selection and rep range, but a deliberate RIR 4–5 (novice/technique dosing per the position statement).

ovelser.navn: Hoftehengsler uten vekt (hendene glir langs lårene)

blokker.formatNavn: Sets × reps · RIR 4–5, full rest and hinge focus

beskrivelse: The ACSM recipe as intended: heavy sets of 8 reps near fatigue with proper rest. Four big exercises, the whole body – short, heavy and measurable.

kilde.navn: ACSM Position Stand: Progression Models in Resistance Training (ACSM, 2009/2026)
kilde.notat: 'Volitional fatigue' in the guidelines is translated to RIR 1–3 for a safe solo session; the rests follow ACSM's 2–3 min recommendation for multi-joint exercises, trimmed toward 2 min to keep the duration in check. Bench optional – floor press works.

beskrivelse: Pavel's famous recipe in its simplest form: powerful swings and slow get-ups, never to fatigue. Two exercises that build the whole body – and you leave feeling rested.

kilde.navn: Kettlebell Simple & Sinister-struktur (Pavel Tsatsouline/StrongFirst, 2013/2019)
kilde.notat: Only the generic swing+get-up structure; the book's weight progression and test standards are left out as proprietary. Pavel prescribes training far from fatigue (the talk test must be passed) – hence an easy session despite the high technique demand.

blokker.formatNavn: Light clock · RIR ≥ 4, the talk test must be passed

beskrivelse: The classic 5×5 session as Reg Park wrote it in 1960: three big barbell lifts, five heavy sets of five with long rests. Timeless strength building – heavy, simple and honest.

kilde.navn: 5×5 (Reg Park, 1960)
kilde.notat: A generic 5×5 structure credited to Park (not StrongLifts' proprietary progression). Park advised against training to failure – work sets at RIR 1–3. Deadlift reduced to 3×5 to keep the session ≤ 40 min.

beskrivelse: A calm round through the whole body with mild, static holds. Perfect after a workout or a long day – breathe easy and let the tension release bit by bit.

kilde.navn: Statisk tøying etter trening (NHS-tradisjon/Bob Anderson, ACSM-dosering)
kilde.notat: The NHS session dosed per ACSM: static holds of 30 s, 1–2 reps per muscle group. Mild tension, never pain.

ovelser.navn: Hamstring stående (hæl frem, len hoften bak)

blokker.formatNavn: Upper body and neck — static holds 30 s

beskrivelse: Simple poses held for a long time – here it's about sinking in and staying. Calm breath, gravity does the work. Deep and demanding, but never forced.

kilde.navn: Yin yoga grunnserie (Paul Grilley/Bernie Clark)
kilde.notat: Beginner poses from Clark's beginner flows, holds shortened to 2–3 min. Come out of the pose if you feel numbness or sharp pain.

blokker.formatNavn: Yin hold 2–3 min — passive, rounded back ok
Exit — legs up along the wall

beskrivelse: A mild, systematic opening of the hips and thoracic spine – the areas that stiffen most from sitting. A little setup per position, but calm and good loading.

kilde.navn: Moderne mobilitetstøying, ACSM-dosert (90/90 fra Functional Range-miljøet)
kilde.notat: 90/90 and pigeon dosed per ACSM: static 30–45 s, 2 reps. The balancing quad stretch placed here (medium skill).

beskrivelse: A quiet yin journey for the body's side lines, shoulders and chest with long, passive holds. Sink in, breathe calmly and let gravity open the areas that rarely get time.

kilde.navn: Yin yoga side-/skulderposisjoner (Paul Grilley/Bernie Clark)
kilde.notat: An approved synthesis change: the session is reangled from Grilley's classic hip series to a side/shoulder/chest series (bananasana, melting heart, side fold, twisted roots — all documented yin poses at yinyoga.com/Clark) to avoid duplicating the yoga category's yin hip session. Hold times of 2–3 min retained.

blokker.formatNavn: Yin hold 2–3 min — find the edge, stay there

ovelser.navn: Smeltende hjerte (anahatasana, knestående, brystet mot gulvet)
Sidefold over bredt bein (sidebøy over strakt bein)
Twisted roots (liggende vridning m/ bøyde, flettede knær)

beskrivelse: Precise technique, mild sensation: you lift the leg actively and give just a small pull at the end. Short 2-second holds outsmart the stretch reflex – clever stretching for body nerds.

kilde.navn: Active Isolated Stretching (Aaron Mattes, 1970-tallet)
kilde.notat: Mattes' method formatted as reps: activate the antagonist, assist lightly with the strap for the last few cm, hold only 2 s, 10 reps. A towel can replace the strap.

blokker.formatNavn: AIS lower body — lift actively, light pull assist, hold 2 s

ovelser.navn: Adduktor liggende m/ strikk (benet ut til siden)
Hoftebøyer sideliggende (hæl mot sete, aktiv hofteekstensjon)

blokker.formatNavn: AIS chest — arm actively out and down, hold 2 s

beskrivelse: The toughest session in the category: real muscle contractions against the stretch open new range of motion, before you test the depth in the splits progression. Warm up first!

kilde.navn: PNF hold-relax (Kabat & Knott, 1940–50-tallet)
kilde.notat: Self-administered hold-relax: isometric contraction 6 s (about 50% effort), release 3 s, deeper static stretch 30 s. A splits finish (GMB tradition) folded in. Breathe steadily – don't hold your breath during the contractions.

blokker.formatNavn: PNF cycle: stretch 10 s → press against 6 s (50%) → release 3 s → deeper stretch 30 s

ovelser.navn: Hamstring liggende (hender bak lår, press hælen ned)
ovelser.dose: 3×(6 s press + 30 s strekk) per side

ovelser.navn: Hoftebøyer i dyp utfall (press bakre kne mot gulvet)
ovelser.dose: 3×(6 s press + 30 s strekk) per side

ovelser.navn: Adduktor sittende v/ vegg (press knærne mot hendene)
Legg mot vegg (press tåballen mot veggen)
ovelser.dose: 2×(6 s press + 30 s strekk) per side

blokker.formatNavn: Splits finish — as deep as you can control

beskrivelse: The easy base ride that Norwegian research is built on. Hold conversational pace the whole way — if it feels too easy, you're doing it right. These are the hours that build the engine.

kilde.navn: Polarisert 80/20-trening (Stephen Seiler, Universitetet i Agder; Seiler & Kjerland 2006)
kilde.notat: The original study followed cross-country skiers; Seiler himself has generalized the model to cycling. Easier: shorten the Z2 block to 15 min. Tougher: extend Z2 to 35 min — never harder, just longer.

beskrivelse: The original Tabata itself — born on the bike in 1996. Four minutes that feel like an eternity, but all you have to do is follow the beep and give it everything. Short, brutal and done.

kilde.navn: Tabata-protokollen (Izumi Tabata et al., 1996)
kilde.notat: The original 8×20/10 on a bike ergometer kept; warm-up/spin-down per the lab practice from the 1996 study. Beginner: 6 rounds at very hard instead of all-out. Experienced: two sets with 5 min easy between.

beskrivelse: Easy, but with discipline: three steady blocks where the job is to hold the zone stable by feel — neither sneaking it up on the hills nor letting it drop. The foundation of all power-based training.

kilde.navn: Level 2 Endurance, 7-sonesystemet (Andrew Coggan & Hunter Allen, Training and Racing with a Power Meter)
kilde.notat: L2 (56–75% FTP) guided by feel/RPE instead of a power meter. Easier: 3×6 min in lower Z2. Tougher: 3×10 min in upper Z2 (73–75% FTP) — still conversational pace.

faser.navn: Steady Z2 — hold the zone stable, cadence 85–95

beskrivelse: Norway's most famous interval session, straight from the NTNU research. Four steady, heavy efforts where the art is pacing: as strong on the fourth effort as on the first.

kilde.navn: 4×4-intervaller (Jan Helgerud & Jan Hoff, NTNU; Helgerud et al. 2007)
kilde.notat: The 2007 study was run-based; the 4×4 is recommended on the bike by the NTNU group itself and the heart-rate control is identical. Easier: 3×4 min at 85–90% max heart rate. Tougher: 93–95% of max heart rate and active recoveries in Z2.

parametre.sone: Z1→Z2 · last minute up toward Z3

faser.navn: Hard effort @ 90–95% max heart rate — steady load

beskrivelse: Low load, high precision: here you train your legs to spin fast and quiet. The goal is a silky-smooth pedal stroke at 110+ — your upper body should stay completely still.

kilde.navn: Kadens-driller / spin-ups (bane- og landeveistradisjon; CTS/Carmichael, TrainingPeaks)
kilde.notat: Stop increasing the spin-up if you start bouncing in the saddle. Easier: 4 spin-ups and a cap at 105 rpm. Tougher: 8 spin-ups, or single-leg pedaling 2×(1 min per leg) on a trainer.

faser.navn: Spin-up: build from 90 to 110–120+ rpm in an easy gear

beskrivelse: The Norwegian interval favorite from Lillehammer: 30 seconds on, 15 off — the precision lies in hitting the same watts effort after effort. Tough, smart and very effective.

kilde.navn: 30/15-protokollen (Bent Rønnestad, Høgskolen i Innlandet; Rønnestad et al. 2020)
kilde.notat: Reduced from 3×13 to 2×13 efforts to keep it at 38 min. Easier: 2×10 efforts at RPE 8. Original/tougher: the full study protocol 3×13 (about 50 min) for the experienced.

parametre.sone: Z1→Z2 · finish with 3×(30 s Z4 / 30 s easy)

beskrivelse: A safe and gentle start to strength training, straight from the British health authorities. You use only your body and a chair – perfect for building the habit and feeling your body get stronger.

kilde.navn: Strength exercises (NHS/HSE, offentlig retningslinje)
kilde.notat: The NHS exercise selection assembled into one guided session with a warm-up and cool-down; easy pace, RIR ≥ 4 and 60 s rest between sets. Easier: sit-to-stand with hand support and half the reps. Harder: squats without chair support, 10 reps per set.

blokker.formatNavn: Sets × reps · easy pace, RIR ≥ 4, 60 s rest

beskrivelse: The legendary 11-minute program from the Royal Canadian Air Force. Simple exercises, high tempo – you chase your own numbers and feel your heart rate climb from the first minute.

kilde.navn: 5BX Plan, Chart 1 (Dr. Bill Orban / Royal Canadian Air Force, 1961)
kilde.notat: The original is a daily 11-min program; here framed with a warm-up and cool-down. Move up to the next level when you hit the rep targets without being completely wiped out. Easier: brisk marching instead of running, push-ups against a wall. Harder: aim for the upper rep targets or move up a chart level.

faser.navn: Push-ups, on the knees if needed (target 6–12)
Run in place, ~200–400 steps + 10 jumping lunges every 75th step

beskrivelse: Build toward one hundred push-ups with the classic 5-set structure – without emptying the tank. An easy pace and perfect technique mean you come back stronger next session.

kilde.navn: Hundred Pushups (Steve Speirs, 2009) + Grease the Groove (Pavel Tsatsouline)
kilde.notat: Speirs' final 'max reps' set is swapped for stopping at RIR ≥ 4 (GTG principle). Lunges/plank are an add-on for a well-rounded session, not part of the original. If you managed 11–20 on the initial test: use 10-10-8-6-7. Easier: all sets on the knees or against a bench. Harder: the next column/week – keep RIR ≥ 4.

beskrivelse: The research-based 7-minute workout – here in two rounds for full effect. Twelve exercises at high speed with minimal rest deliver cardio and strength in the same session. Short, tough and incredibly satisfying.

kilde.navn: High-Intensity Circuit Training Using Body Weight (Klika & Jordan, ACSM, 2013)
kilde.notat: To avoid the chair: step-ups → squat jumps, dips → close-grip triceps push-ups (wall sit kept). Doubled to 2 rounds to hit 15–30 min. RPE 7–9. Easier: one round, swap jumps for quick steps. Harder: three rounds or 40 s work / 10 s rest.

blokker.formatNavn: 2 rounds · 30 s work / 10 s rest · RPE 7–9

beskrivelse: The gymnasts' secret: a tight, hollow body line. Calm holds and wall drills build the control that one day becomes a handstand – quality above all.

kilde.navn: Hollow body-progresjon og veggdriller (turntradisjonen; GMB/Nerd Fitness)
kilde.notat: Common property from the gymnastics tradition assembled into one technique session (RIR ≥ 4, 60–90 s rest). Keep your lower back pressed into the floor in the hollow. Easier: bent knees in the hollow, skip the kick-ups. Harder: straight legs and arms overhead, wall holds up toward 45 s.

blokker.formatNavn: Hold times · technique focus, RIR ≥ 4, 60–90 s rest

ovelser.navn: Hollow hold (start med bøyde knær, strekk gradvis)
Vegg-planke / halv veggklatring, magen mot veggen
Valgfritt: rolige oppspark mot vegg, strake armer

beskrivelse: The clock rules, you deliver: four tough minutes on repeat with gymnastic strength and burpees. The faster you work with good form, the more rest you earn. A real test for the advanced.

kilde.navn: EMOM-format (CrossFit-tradisjonen) + gymnastic strength-progresjoner
kilde.notat: A synthesis of the CrossFit EMOM (incl. the burpee EMOM) and established gymnastic progressions. Scale the reps so you always have ≥15 s of rest each minute (RPE 7–9). Easier: EMOM 12, kneeling pike push-ups, pistol to a high box. Harder: EMOM 20, negative handstand push-ups, free pistol squats.

blokker.formatNavn: EMOM 16 · start each minute, the rest is recovery

faser.navn: Min 2: 4–6 pistol squats per leg (box pistol ok)

beskrivelse: Soft movements your body has known since you were a baby. Breathe, nod, roll and rock yourself awake — a perfect gentle start to the day.

kilde.navn: Original Strength Pressing Reset (Tim Anderson & Geoff Neupert, 2013)
kilde.notat: A selection of the core resets in a short morning format. Rolling can be done with a little leg help; rocking with wider knees if the hips are tight; head nods with a smaller range if you feel dizzy.

ovelser.navn: Rulling rygg til mage ledet av arm
Vugging på alle fire, hælene mot setet
Kryssmarsj stående, hånd mot motsatt kne

beskrivelse: Three classic exercises that build a strong, stable back. Hold each position with calm force and steady breath — your back will thank you tomorrow.

kilde.navn: McGill Big 3 (Dr. Stuart McGill, University of Waterloo)
kilde.notat: Cat-cow placed first as McGill himself recommends. Side plank from the knees is the standard start; curl-up without lifting (just brace) for an acute back; shorter holds (8 s) for beginners.

blokker.formatNavn: Big 3 — pyramid holds, short rest between

ovelser.navn: Fuglehund — tegn en firkant med hånden hjem

beskrivelse: A calm round through the world's greatest stretch — hips, thoracic spine and posterior chain in one flowing movement. Perfect before a workout or as a break in your day.

kilde.navn: World's Greatest Stretch / Movement Prep (popularisert av Mark Verstegen, Core Performance, 2004)
kilde.notat: A Movement Prep selection at an easy pace. The back knee on the floor makes the lunge easier; skip the rotation if your shoulder hurts; hands on a block/book instead of the floor.

ovelser.navn: Verdens beste tøyning: utfall, albue mot gulv, rotasjon, hofta bak
90/90-vipp side til side med hendene bak

blokker.formatNavn: Deep squat with easy weight shifts

beskrivelse: Work the hips through internal and external rotation with real effort in the end range. The lifts are heavy for the muscles around the hip — that's the point!

kilde.navn: 90/90 med end-range lift-offs (fysioterapitradisjon, systematisert i Functional Range Conditioning, Dr. Andreo Spina)
kilde.notat: Use your hands behind you for support in the transitions; shorter holds (3 s) and one round if your knee is irritated; a cushion under the seat if the hip is tight.

ovelser.navn: Fremoverlening over fremre legg med lang rygg
Lift-off innadrotasjon: bakre fot løftes, kneet i bakken
Lift-off utadrotasjon: fremre legg løftes fra gulvet
Pigeon push-up: press opp fra fremre legg

beskrivelse: A calm round where each joint gets its daily rotation — slow, precise and with low tension. Oil your joints like a morning coffee for the body.

kilde.navn: Controlled Articular Rotations, CARs (Functional Range Conditioning, Dr. Andreo Spina)
kilde.notat: Low tension (~30%), the rest of the body still. Smaller circles if you feel clicking/pinching; shoulder CARs on all fours give more stability; hip CARs with a hand on the wall for balance.

blokker.formatNavn: The morning round — joint by joint

ovelser.navn: Skulder-CARs stående eller på alle fire

beskrivelse: Full control in the end ranges: shoulders, hips and ankles work with muscular force through large ranges of motion. Demanding — and incredibly good afterward.

kilde.navn: Endrange-kontrolldriller (gymnastikk- og styrketradisjon; hip airplane popularisert av Dr. Stuart McGill)
kilde.notat: A composite flow of established drills. A wider grip or looser band makes dislocates easier; cossack with the heel lifted or hold onto a doorframe; hip airplane with full wall support at first.

blokker.formatNavn: Shoulder dislocates — wide grip, straight arms

ovelser.navn: Dyp knebøy-hold med rotasjonsrekk mot taket
Cossack squat, sakte tempo, full dybde etter evne
Hip airplane fra ettbeinsstående: rotér hofta inn og ut
Knestående til stående overgang uten hender

beskrivelse: Japan's most studied interval form, moved into the living room. Three easy rounds of faster and slower marching – a simple clock, kind to the joints, and you feel the warmth come with no jumping at all.

kilde.navn: Interval Walking Training (Nose & Masuki, Shinshu University, 1999; Nemoto et al. 2007)
kilde.notat: Original: 5 rounds of brisk/easy walking outdoors (~70% VO2max in the fast phase). Here: marching in place, 3 rounds and the top speed toned down to Z2–Z3; the 3/3 clock kept exactly.

faser.navn: Work: brisk march with active arms + light knee lifts (RPE 4–5)

beskrivelse: The researchers behind the 'one-minute workout' showed that short, tough minutes deliver big results. Eight hard efforts of one minute each – simple exercises, honest effort, and good rest between each round.

kilde.navn: Gibala «practical model» (Little et al., McMaster University, 2010)
kilde.notat: Original: 8–12 × 60 s / 75 s on a bike ergometer; here 8 rounds of simple bodyweight exercises in place. The 60/75 clock kept exactly; 8 rounds chosen to hit 20–25 min.

faser.navn: Work: brisk march with arm press (RPE 7–8)
Work: your choice of favorite exercise (RPE 7–8)

beskrivelse: The mother of circuit training from Leeds in 1953 – in a Sunday edition. Eight stations, half a minute on and half a minute off, the whole body moving without your heart rate taking off completely. A perfect everyday session.

kilde.navn: Sirkeltrening (Morgan & Adamson, University of Leeds, 1953)
kilde.notat: The original is hard station training; here toned down to RPE 4–5 following documented low-threshold HIIT practice (Harvard Health). A 30/30 clock, 8 stations, 2 rounds with a 60 s set break.

blokker.formatNavn: 2 rounds × 8 stations (30 s on / 30 s off)

faser.navn: Work: squats at an easy pace
Work: standing row with shoulder squeeze
Work: easy march with diagonal arm reaches

beskrivelse: The original 20/10 protocol from 1996 – four minutes that feel like forty. Two blocks, four familiar exercises and the world's most famous interval clock. Short, brutal and gloriously done.

kilde.navn: Tabata-protokollen (Izumi Tabata, National Institute of Fitness and Sports, 1996)
kilde.notat: Original: 7–8 × 20/10 on a bike ergometer at 170% VO2max – impossible with bodyweight; intensity is therefore given as RPE 8–9 (established full-body practice, cf. the Tabata 2019 review). The 20/10 clock kept exactly, two blocks.

blokker.formatNavn: March → knee lifts → easy squats → light jumps

faser.navn: Work: high knees at max pace

parametre.sone: Z1 · heart rate all the way down

beskrivelse: The Copenhagen protocol in a soft gear: thirty easy, twenty brisker and ten quick seconds – over and over. The art is hitting each gear precisely, not going hard. Wonderfully rhythmic!

kilde.navn: 10-20-30-protokollen (Gunnarsson & Bangsbo, Københavns Universitet, 2012)
kilde.notat: Original: running with a sprint in the 10-second gear; here marching/knee lifts in place with a toned-down top gear (RPE 5, easy interval). The 30-20-10 pace changes and the 5-minute blocks with 2 min rest kept exactly.

faser.navn: Gear 3: light knee lifts, brisk pace (RPE 5)
Gear 3: light knee lifts, brisk pace (RPE 5)
Gear 3: light knee lifts, brisk pace (RPE 5)
Gear 3: light knee lifts, brisk pace (RPE 5)
Gear 3: light knee lifts, brisk pace (RPE 5)
Gear 3: light knee lifts, brisk pace (RPE 5)
Gear 3: light knee lifts, brisk pace (RPE 5)
Gear 3: light knee lifts, brisk pace (RPE 5)
Gear 3: light knee lifts, brisk pace (RPE 5)
Gear 3: light knee lifts, brisk pace (RPE 5)
Gear 3: light knee lifts, brisk pace (RPE 5)

beskrivelse: The clock is the boss: a new task every minute, and the rest is what you can earn. Four explosive exercises across sixteen minutes – the more evenly you pace, the better it goes. A real treat!

kilde.navn: EMOM – Every Minute On the Minute (vektløftertradisjon, systematisert i CrossFit)
kilde.notat: Not a single study – an established training format. The reps are chosen so you earn at least 15 s of rest per minute (aim for RPE 7–9); scale down to 6/10/24/8 if the rest disappears.

blokker.formatNavn: March → knee lifts → squats → 2–3 easy burpees
EMOM 16 min: done = the rest of the minute is rest

faser.navn: Minute 1: 8 burpees – rest for the remainder of the minute
Minute 2: 12 squat jumps – rest for the remainder of the minute
Minute 3: 30 mountain climbers total – rest the remainder
Minute 4: 10 jump lunges (alternating legs) – rest the remainder

parametre.sone: Z1 · heart rate all the way down

beskrivelse: The body's own calm button: two inhales through the nose and one long sigh out. Five minutes is all it takes to lower your shoulders after a long day — no counting, just breathing.

kilde.navn: Syklisk sukk / cyclic sighing (Balban m.fl., Stanford — Huberman & Spiegel, 2023)
kilde.notat: The original is count-free; the 3-0-6-0 cadence is a guide (the double inhale ≈ an easy inhale + a short top-up inhale). 3 min of after-rest added to Balban's 5-min protocol. No breath holds; stop if you feel dizzy. Easier: 3 min of sighing + 2 min of rest. Deeper: lengthen the exhale, or do it morning and evening.

blokker.formatNavn: Cyclic sighing — double inhale (two in through the nose), long sigh out

parametre.sone: Completely natural breath · feel the calm in your body

beskrivelse: A calm series where the wall and the floor do the work. Legs up, breath down — the body empties heavy legs and the nervous system gets the message that the danger has passed. Take care with high blood pressure.

kilde.navn: Viparita Karani / restorativ yoga (Iyengar-tradisjonen; Judith Hanson Lasater)
kilde.notat: Hold times set for 25 min total; calm nasal breathing throughout. Come out slowly if you feel dizzy; avoid the inversion with uncontrolled high blood pressure or glaucoma. Easier: 5 min per pose, knees slightly bent. Deeper: 15 min legs up the wall with a cushion under the lower back, up toward 35 min.

ovelser.navn: Viparita Karani — beina opp veggen, setet inntil vegg
Supta Baddha Konasana — fotsålene samlet, knær ut til siden
Barnets stilling — pannen mot matten, armene langs siden

blokker.formatNavn: Savasana and a calm transition out

ovelser.navn: Savasana — flatt på ryggen, la kroppen bli tung
Rull til siden, sitt opp langsomt

beskrivelse: The square that calms the mind: four seconds in, hold, out, hold. The same tool elite operators use to find calm — a perfect break in the middle of the day.

kilde.navn: Boks-pust / box breathing (Mark Divine, US Navy SEALs / SEALFIT)
kilde.notat: 10 min continuous (about 37 rounds), all breathing through the nose; empty your lungs completely first. Mild breath holds — never in water or behind the wheel; if you feel dizzy, drop the holds and breathe normally. Easier: a 3-3-3-3 cadence or 5 min. Deeper: 5-5-5-5 once the 4-count feels effortless.

blokker.formatNavn: Box breathing 4-4-4-4 — mild breath holds, never in water

beskrivelse: A quiet journey through the body from toes to head. You don't have to perform anything — just notice what's there, and let the tensions release along the way.

kilde.navn: Kroppsskann / body scan, MBSR (Jon Kabat-Zinn, UMass Medical School, 1979)
kilde.notat: A generic phase structure, not a verbatim script; shortened from the classic 30–45 min to 25 min. If your mind wanders: notice it kindly and return. Natural breath throughout. Easier: 15 min with larger body regions. Deeper: the full 40-min MBSR length, toe by toe.

blokker.formatNavn: Body scan — lying down, toes to head

faser.navn: Landing — points of contact with the ground, breathe into the belly
Left leg — toes, foot, ankle, calf, knee, thigh
Right leg — toes, foot, ankle, calf, knee, thigh
The whole body as one — easy finish

beskrivelse: Breathe at the body's own resonance — five and a half breaths per minute. Steady as a wave, in and out, until heart and breath swing in sync and calm spreads.

kilde.navn: Koherent pust / coherent breathing (Brown & Gerbarg; resonansfrekvens Lehrer/Gevirtz)
kilde.notat: 12 min at 5.5 breaths/min (about 66 breaths); soft nasal breathing without strain, let the inhale glide seamlessly into the exhale. No breath holds — safe for most people. Easier: 6/min (5 s in / 5 s out) or 8 min. Deeper: extend toward 20 min.

blokker.formatNavn: Coherent breathing — steady, seamless rhythm with no pauses

beskrivelse: A half-hour in the borderland between waking and sleep. The attention is led systematically through the body while the nervous system recharges — deep rest without sleeping.

kilde.navn: Yoga nidra / NSDR (Swami Satyananda Saraswati, Bihar School of Yoga; Huberman)
kilde.notat: A generic Satyananda phase structure — no reproduction of protected scripts. Demanding for the attention, not the body; it's fine to fall asleep, but the goal is waking deep rest. Easier: 20 min without the opposites phase. Deeper: 40–45 min with two rounds of body rotation and longer silence.

faser.navn: Settling in — let the body grow heavy, long exhales
Rotation of awareness — quickly and systematically through the body
Breath awareness — count exhales backward from 27
Quiet rest — just being, no task
Return — repeat the intention, small movements, sit up

beskrivelse: A calm full-body session that builds the foundation: one exercise from each pattern – press, squat, hip hinge and core. Perfect for building the habit and feeling your body get stronger.

kilde.navn: NHS – Strength and flexibility exercises
kilde.notat: A balanced full-body session assembled from the four basic patterns in the Learn track.

blokker.formatNavn: Sets × reps · easy pace, 60 s rest

beskrivelse: The lower body gets a workout: squats, lunges and hip hinges build strong legs and firm glutes – with no weights at all. Short rest and good technique deliver both strength and heart rate.

kilde.navn: ACSM – Resistance Training for Health
kilde.notat: A lower-body-focused bodyweight session built on the progressions in the Learn track.

blokker.formatNavn: Stretch out the back of the body

beskrivelse: Build a strong and balanced upper body with press and pull in pairs. An easy pace and full control mean you come back stronger next time.

kilde.navn: Dan John – «Push, Pull, Hinge, Squat» balanseprinsipp
kilde.notat: Press and pull balanced in pairs, with the grips learned in the Learn track.

beskrivelse: A tight and focused core session built on anti-movement: keep your back steady while arms and legs work. Quality on each hold beats quantity every time.

kilde.navn: Stuart McGill – «Big Three» kjerneøvelser
kilde.notat: Anti-movement and back-friendly holds, as learned in the 'Strong Core' unit.

beskrivelse: A complete session for the advanced: full strength, core and the first skills in one. Easy pace, high quality – control through the whole movement.

kilde.navn: Gymnastic strength-tradisjonen (GMB / Nerd Fitness)
kilde.notat: Full strength, core and skills assembled from the 'Beyond Bodyweight' section.

beskrivelse: A kind session for stiff days: mobilize the joints, wake the body gently and stretch well. Perfect as active rest or a soft start to a training week.

kilde.navn: Functional Range Conditioning – CARs & mobilitet
kilde.notat: Active mobility and easy stretching, as learned in the warm-up and stretching sections.

beskrivelse: The foundation of all fitness: one long, easy walk in zone 1 where you can talk the whole way. Time on your feet counts more than pace — build endurance hour by hour.

kilde.navn: Lavintensiv utholdenhetstrening / LISS (Seiler, 2010; Helsedirektoratet 2022)
kilde.notat: A long, continuous low-intensity walk split into three timer phases. The benefit comes from the duration in the low zone, not from pace; the talk test keeps the intensity down.

blokker.formatNavn: Long, steady walk at conversational pace
Easy walk — let your breath settle

beskrivelse: A short, easy walk right after a meal. Research shows that just fifteen minutes of walking after eating blunts the blood-sugar spike — the simplest habit with a big health payoff.

kilde.navn: Gange etter måltid og blodsukker (Buffey et al., Sports Medicine 2022)
kilde.notat: The meta-analysis finding (light walking after eating lowers the glucose response, most at up to ~15 min) operationalized as one easy 15-min walk framed by a short warm-up/cool-down.

blokker.formatNavn: Easy start right out the door
Ease off the last few minutes

beskrivelse: Find a long staircase or steep hill and turn it into a piece of gym equipment. Up at a brisk pace, easy down as recovery — simple, tough and seriously hard on both legs and cardio.

kilde.navn: Kort intens trappegang (Allison et al., McMaster University, 2017)
kilde.notat: McMaster's 'stair snacks' (short, intense stair efforts) adapted for walking: 6×(1 min up / 2.5 min easy down) framed by a warm-up and cool-down. Intensity is guided by feel (RPE 7–8).

blokker.formatNavn: Easy walk over to the stairs
6× up at a brisk pace / easy down

faser.navn: Brisk walk up the stairs — active arms, short steps
Easy walk down — catch your breath

beskrivelse: Interval walking that steps up and back down: 1-2-3-2-1 minutes at a brisk pace with easy recoveries between. The pyramid keeps your mind engaged and builds speed without becoming monotonous.

kilde.navn: Pyramideintervaller (klassisk intervallstruktur; jf. Billat, 2001)
kilde.notat: A classic pyramid session (1-2-3-2-1 min work with easy recoveries between) adapted for walking. The intensity is kept in the brisk-walking zone (RPE 6–7) and guided by feel.

faser.navn: Brisk walk 3 min — top of the pyramid

beskrivelse: Slow, sensory walking in a forest or park — what the Japanese call forest bathing. Walk slowly, breathe deeply and notice sounds, light and scent. Documented to calm stress and heart rate.

kilde.navn: Shinrin-yoku / skogsbad (Park et al., 2010; Hansen et al., 2017)
kilde.notat: The Japanese forest-bathing practice (slow, mindful nature walking) framed as one easy 30-min walk. The health effects (lower heart rate and cortisol) come from the slow pace and the nature exposure, not from intensity.

blokker.formatNavn: Slow, mindful walking — feel free to stop and pause
Stand still, breathe deeply and check in

beskrivelse: Find a steady, steep hill and walk it hard uphill over and over. The climb builds power in the glutes and calves and raises your fitness — the easy walks down are all the rest you get.

kilde.navn: Motbakkeintervaller (klassisk bakketrening; jf. Barnes & Kilding, 2015)
kilde.notat: Hill intervals adapted for brisk walking: 5×(2 min hard up / easy down). The hill controls the intensity and spares the joints compared to a flat sprint; RPE 8 during the effort.

blokker.formatNavn: Easy walk over to the hill

faser.navn: Hard uphill walking — push the pace up the climb

beskrivelse: Start easy and dial up the pace bit by bit, so you run fastest at the end. The best habit a runner can learn — you finish strong instead of blowing up, and you learn to ration your effort.

kilde.navn: Progressivt løp / negative splits (pacing-forskning; Foster et al.)
kilde.notat: A progressive structure (easy → moderate → threshold) split into three equal phases. Pacing is guided by the talk test since the app doesn't measure speed; the final phase in the threshold zone (RPE 7).

beskrivelse: An easy jog with a few short strides at the end, where you smoothly build the pace up toward an almost-sprint. It makes your stride quicker and your form lighter — without wearing you out.

kilde.navn: Stigningsløp (strides) i utholdenhetstrening (Daniels' Running Formula, 2013)
kilde.notat: Classic strides (6×~20 s of gradual acceleration with a full walking recovery) placed after an easy base run. The efforts are technical, not maximal, with full recovery between each.

faser.navn: Stride — smoothly build up toward a fast, light pace

beskrivelse: Four controlled efforts at a 'comfortably hard' threshold pace with short recoveries. The threshold session moves the limit of how long you can hold speed — a cornerstone of all serious run training.

kilde.navn: Terskelintervaller (Seiler polarisert modell, 2010; norsk terskeltradisjon)
kilde.notat: Classic threshold efforts (4×5 min at threshold, 1 min rest) framed by a warm-up and cool-down. Pace is guided by the talk test (single words, not max); RPE 6–7.

faser.navn: Threshold pace — comfortably hard and steady

navn: Long Run at an Easy Pace
beskrivelse: The week's longest run, kept deliberately easy in zone 2 the whole way. The long run builds endurance, a stronger heart and a mind that can handle being on your feet for a long time. The pace should feel almost too easy.

kilde.navn: Langkjøring / long slow distance (Seiler & Tønnessen, 2009)
kilde.notat: A classic long run kept in the low zone (Z1–Z2) the whole way. A continuous run split into three timer phases; the talk test keeps the pace down. RPE 3–4.

blokker.formatNavn: Long, steady jog at conversational pace

parametre.sone: Z2 · full sentences the whole way

beskrivelse: The next step from your first jogs: longer jogging efforts and shorter walking breaks. You keep building on the walk-run method toward jogging continuously — safely and gradually.

kilde.navn: Couch to 5K, uke 3 (Josh Clark, 1996; NHS Better Health)
kilde.notat: The Week 3 structure (2 rounds of 90 s jog + 90 s walk + 3 min jog + 3 min walk) used unchanged from NHS/c25k; phase texts rewritten in our own words, no proprietary coaching text reused.

parametre.sone: Brisk walk · warm up the legs

blokker.formatNavn: 2× (90 s jog / 90 s walk / 3 min jog / 3 min walk)

beskrivelse: Short, powerful sprints up a steep hill with a full walking recovery down. Hill sprints build explosive strength and better running form with minimal injury risk — the hill slows you down naturally.

kilde.navn: Bakkespurter / hill sprints (jf. Barnes & Kilding om løpsøkonomi, 2015)
kilde.notat: Short, near-maximal hill sprints (8×~15 s with a full walking recovery down) after a thorough warm-up. The hill limits speed and impact, so it delivers strength and technique with low injury risk. RPE 9 during the effort.

blokker.formatNavn: Easy jog and a few strides
8× 15 s hill sprint / walk easy down

faser.navn: Hill sprint — powerfully up with high knees
Walk easy back to start — full rest

beskrivelse: A calm morning flow that wakes the back, hips and breath without rushing. It softens the body and sets a good tone for the day — perfect before breakfast.

kilde.navn: Morgen-vinyasa / halv solhilsen (Krishnamacharya-linjen)
kilde.notat: A calm waking sequence with cat-cow, half sun salutation and a soft finish. Bend your knees freely in the forward fold; take fewer rounds if needed. One movement per breath.

ovelser.beskrivelse: Rolige runder, én bevegelse per pust.

beskrivelse: Yoga's most famous flow: six rounds of Surya Namaskar A where breath and movement melt together. It builds heat, strength and a calm, steady rhythm.

kilde.navn: Surya Namaskara A (K. Pattabhi Jois, Ashtanga-tradisjonen)
kilde.notat: Traditional rounds of Sun A, one movement per breath. Chaturanga on the knees and cobra instead of upward dog as needed. Harder: more rounds; easier: rest in downward dog between rounds.

ovelser.beskrivelse: Seks runder, én bevegelse per pust.

beskrivelse: A powerful standing flow: sun salutations build heat, then strong warrior poses that hold it. You'll feel both strength in the legs and calm in the breath afterward.

kilde.navn: Stående vinyasa-flyt (Virabhadrasana-sekvens, Iyengar/Ashtanga-tradisjonen)
kilde.notat: Sun Salutation A as a warm-up, then a standing warrior block, 4 rounds (2 per side), 5 breaths per pose. Shorten the stance if your knee wobbles; shorter holds if you're fatigued.

ovelser.beskrivelse: Rolige runder for å bli varm.
Fire runder, bytt side hver runde. 5 pust per stilling (3 i fredfull kriger).

beskrivelse: For those who want to challenge their balance: chair, figure-4, side plank and crow prep build steadiness and core strength. It requires focus and a steady gaze.

kilde.navn: Balanse- og armbalanse-yoga (Iyengar-alignment; drishti fra Ashtanga)
kilde.notat: Sun salutation as a warm-up, then balance and core poses with short holds and full rest. Train near a wall if needed; lower your hips in the side plank if the line breaks.

ovelser.beskrivelse: Rolige runder for å bli varm.

navn: Yin Evening Yoga – Back and Hips
beskrivelse: Long, calm holds that release tension in the back and hips after a long day. The breath does the work, not the muscles — lovely as preparation for sleep.

kilde.navn: Yin yoga – kveldsserie (Paul Grilley / Bernie Clark)
kilde.notat: 2–3 min holds with 30–60 sec of rest between. Use a cushion in swan and under the hips; never sharp pain — find 60–70% of your max and stay there. Shorter holds (90 sec) for beginners.

blokker.formatNavn: Yin holds – back and hips (rest 30–60 s between)

beskrivelse: Mild, restorative yoga that calms the nervous system all the way down. Quiet poses and deep breathing loosen the day and get you ready for sleep.

kilde.navn: Restorativ yoga / avspenning (B.K.S. Iyengar-tradisjonen; Judith Lasater)
kilde.notat: Calm, supported poses with long holds and deep breathing. Use cushions and blankets for full support. Stop if you feel discomfort; extend savasana as long as you like.

beskrivelse: A calm round of controlled joint circles from neck to ankles. It oils every joint and wakes the body gently — the perfect start to the day.

kilde.navn: Daily CARs-rutine (Andreo Spina – Functional Range Conditioning)
kilde.notat: One round of CARs through the major joints, slow and controlled. A smaller range if you feel stiff or dizzy; one joint at a time, full control through the whole circle.

beskrivelse: An antidote to screen posture: loosen the stiff thoracic spine and open the shoulders. You'll stand taller and breathe more freely afterward.

kilde.navn: Thoracic mobility-rutine (Kelly Starrett – «Becoming a Supple Leopard»)
kilde.notat: Focus on thoracic rotation and extension plus shoulder opening. Go wider on the band if your shoulders are stiff; keep your lower back stable and move from the thoracic spine.

beskrivelse: Active hip mobility in every direction: rotation, lateral movement and control. Demanding and incredibly freeing for the hips and lower back.

kilde.navn: Aktiv hoftemobilitet (FRC 90/90 + hip airplane, Andreo Spina)
kilde.notat: Active rotation training for the hips with 90/90 and hip airplane. Stay within a pain-free range; use a hand on the wall in the hip airplane until your balance is dialed in.

beskrivelse: Build toward the human's natural resting position: the deep squat with heels on the floor. It opens ankles, knees and hips all at once.

kilde.navn: Deep squat-mobilitet (Ido Portal-inspirert; ankelmobilitet-arbeid)
kilde.notat: A progression toward a relaxed deep squat. Hold onto a doorframe or lift the heels slightly if your ankles are stiff; build time at the bottom gradually, a little each day.

beskrivelse: Roll, crawl, rock and rise — the natural fundamental patterns your body was built for. It builds coordination, control and full-body mobility.

kilde.navn: Bevegelsesflyt / grunnmønstre (Original Strength; Sitting-Rising Test)
kilde.notat: Floor-based fundamental patterns (rolling, crawling, rising). Do the rolling with a little leg help at first; rise with one hand for support if your balance falters, and build toward doing it without hands.

beskrivelse: Calm, soft movements that loosen the day's tension in the neck, back and hips. Gentle and good before bedtime.

kilde.navn: Rolig kveldsmobilitet (Original Strength resets + hofteåpning)
kilde.notat: Soft, calm movements at a low tempo. A small range is enough; keep the breath long and slow the pace toward the end to calm the nervous system before sleep.

navn: Full Body A – The Big Lifts
beskrivelse: A classic full-body session around the big three: squat, bench press and row. It hits the whole body in one session — the backbone of a simple, effective strength program.

kilde.navn: Full-body A (StrongLifts / Starting Strength-mal)
kilde.notat: Full body with squat, press and pull. Reps set conservatively (RIR 2–3) for easy intensity; swap the barbell for dumbbells if needed, and use safety arms in the bench press.

navn: Full Body B – Deadlift and Press
beskrivelse: The counterpart to Full Body A: deadlift, overhead shoulder press and Romanian deadlift. Alternate between A and B through the week for a complete program.

kilde.navn: Full-body B (StrongLifts / Starting Strength-mal)
kilde.notat: Full body with deadlift, overhead press and hinge. Start the deadlift light and build technique before weight; RIR 1–2 on the last set. Use a barbell or dumbbells depending on what you have.

beskrivelse: A pure pressing session for chest, shoulders and arms. Bench press and shoulder press as the main courses, with a curl for dessert — build your upper-body pushing power.

kilde.navn: Push-dag (push/pull/legs-splitt)
kilde.notat: A press-focused day in a push/pull/legs split. Set your shoulders and use safety arms/a spotter in the bench press; keep the curl strict with no swing. RIR 2–3.

navn: Pull Day – Back and Posterior Chain
beskrivelse: A pulling session for a strong back and posterior chain. Deadlift, row and Romanian deadlift build posture, grip strength and the entire back of the body.

kilde.navn: Pull-dag (push/pull/legs-splitt)
kilde.notat: A pull-focused day in a push/pull/legs split. Keep your back neutral in all pulls and hinges; squeeze the shoulder blades in the row. RIR 2–3.

beskrivelse: One kettlebell, the whole body. Swing, goblet squat, Turkish get-up and carry in a flowing complex that builds power, conditioning and control.

kilde.navn: Kettlebell-kompleks (Pavel Tsatsouline / StrongFirst)
kilde.notat: A full-body kettlebell session with the swing and get-up as its backbone. The power in the swing comes from the hips; learn the Turkish get-up light first. Rest as needed between rounds. RIR 2.

beskrivelse: The timeless strength recipe: five sets of five reps on the big barbell lifts. Heavy, simple and extremely effective for raw strength — add a little weight each week.

kilde.navn: 5×5-styrkeprogram (Bill Starr / StrongLifts 5×5)
kilde.notat: A classic 5×5 on the big barbell lifts. Add a little weight when all the sets succeed; RIR 1–2. Use a rack and safety arms, and start lighter than you think to build technique.

beskrivelse: The original recipe in practice: four brutal minutes of 20 seconds all-out and 10 seconds rest. Two Tabata blocks, two exercises, one highly effective session.

kilde.navn: Tabata-protokollen (Tabata et al., 1996)
kilde.notat: Two classic Tabata blocks (20/10 × 8) each with its own exercise and a rest between. The original was on a bike ergometer at very high intensity; here with bodyweight exercises. Easier: swap jumps for quick squats.

faser.navn: Work: squat jumps — all you've got
Work: mountain climbers at max pace

beskrivelse: 'As many rounds as possible' on a short, powerful circuit. You control the pace yourself for twelve minutes — the more rounds, the harder the session. Honest and electric.

kilde.navn: AMRAP-sirkel (CrossFit-metodikk)
kilde.notat: As-many-rounds-as-possible of a fixed circuit within a set time window. Build the pace up safely; note your round count and try to beat it next time. Scale by reducing reps or dropping the jumps.

blokker.formatNavn: Easy march and light knee lifts
AMRAP 12 min — as many rounds as possible

beskrivelse: A safe way into interval training: short, brisk efforts with plenty of rest and no jumping. You feel the alternation between hard and rest without being overwhelmed.

kilde.navn: Lavintensiv intervall-intro (ACSM-tilpasset)
kilde.notat: A gentle intro with short work intervals (30 s) and long rests (60 s), low-threshold exercises with no jumping. Increase the pace and shorten the rest as your fitness builds.

faser.navn: Work: high knees at a brisk pace (RPE 6)

beskrivelse: The Danish research concept: each minute goes from easy to medium to all-out in ten seconds. Self-regulating and surprisingly effective on low volume.

kilde.navn: 10-20-30-konseptet (Gunnarsson & Bangsbo, Københavns Universitet, 2012)
kilde.notat: Three series of 5 min (5 × 30/20/10), 2 min easy between series. The max part of 10 s sets the intensity; the rest is comfortable. Can be done walking, running or in place.

faser.navn: 10 s high knees — all you've got (RPE 9)

beskrivelse: Intervals that step up and down: 20-30-40-30-20 seconds of work with equal rest. The pyramid keeps your mind engaged and lets you peak your effort in the middle.

kilde.navn: Pyramideintervaller (klassisk intervallstruktur)
kilde.notat: The work intervals step up and down (20-30-40-30-20 s) with equal-length rests. Alternate between two exercises. Scale by using gentle variations without jumping.

faser.navn: Jumping jacks 40 s — top of the pyramid

beskrivelse: The tough total: all the explosive full-body movements in one hard circuit. Burpees, jumps and mountain climbers in a row — for those who want to feel the burn.

kilde.navn: Helkropps-HIIT-sirkel (bodyweight HIIT)
kilde.notat: Four rounds of a full-body circuit with 40 s work / 20 s rest per exercise, 1 min rest between rounds. Scale down to quick squats and lunges without jumps, and burpees without the push-up/jump.

blokker.formatNavn: Easy march, knee lifts and arm circles

beskrivelse: The backbone of all cycling training: one long, steady ride in zone 2 where you can talk the whole way. Time in the saddle builds the big aerobic engine.

kilde.navn: Langkjøring / sone 2-base (Seiler; Allen & Coggan)
kilde.notat: Continuous zone 2 split into three timer phases. Hold a smooth cadence (85–95 rpm) and conversational pace the whole way; time counts more than speed.

parametre.sone: Z2 · smooth cadence 85–95 rpm, talk the whole way

beskrivelse: Active rest on the bike: very light pedaling that moves blood and loosens the legs without loading them. Perfect the day after a hard session.

kilde.navn: Aktiv restitusjon / recovery spin
kilde.notat: The whole session in zone 1 with low resistance and an easy cadence. It should feel too easy — the point is to move blood, not to train. Stop if your legs get heavy.

parametre.sone: Z1 · low resistance, legs almost unloaded

beskrivelse: A lot of return for the wear: three blocks just below threshold. Sweet spot builds threshold fitness efficiently without crushing you — the load-bearing beam of many plans.

kilde.navn: Sweet Spot Training (Frank Overton / FasCat)
kilde.notat: Three 8-min blocks at 88–94% of FTP with an easy rest between. Hold a steady load and a smooth cadence; build to longer blocks over time.

blokker.formatNavn: Easy pedaling + a couple of spin-ups

beskrivelse: Controlled 'comfortably hard' around threshold. Four efforts that move the limit of how long you can hold speed — the cornerstone of serious cycling training.

kilde.navn: Terskelintervaller (Allen & Coggan; norsk terskeltradisjon)
kilde.notat: Four threshold efforts of 6 min at 95–100% of FTP with 3 min easy between. Hold a steady load through the whole effort — don't start too hard.

faser.navn: Threshold @ 95–100% FTP — steady and comfortably hard

beskrivelse: Power and fitness in one: heavy efforts uphill or in a big gear at low cadence. It builds pedaling force and threshold, and teaches the legs to grind.

kilde.navn: Motbakke-/kraftintervaller (low-cadence force intervals)
kilde.notat: Five efforts of 4 min uphill or in a heavy gear at low cadence (60–70 rpm), threshold load, with easy pedaling between. Stand up in doses when it gets steep.

beskrivelse: Threshold training with a hint of VO2: you alternate between just below and just above threshold with no rest. It teaches the body to handle lactate at speed.

kilde.navn: Over-under-intervaller (klassisk terskelprotokoll)
kilde.notat: Three blocks where you alternate 2 min 'under' (~90% FTP) and 1 min 'over' (~105% FTP) with no rest, with easy pedaling between blocks. Requires good pacing.
